facts.label: THE MARADONA OF THE ARABS
facts.story: Deep in Saudi Arabia's own half against Belgium in 1994, midfielder Saeed Al-Owairan collected a loose ball, and instead of laying it off, set off running. Sixty-nine metres later, having beaten four defenders and rounded the goalkeeper, he slotted home the only goal of the game. FIFA still ranks it among the greatest World Cup goals ever scored, and it earned Al-Owairan a nickname that outlived his career: the Maradona of the Arabs.

facts.label: COLORS OF THE FLAG
facts.story: For nearly three decades, Saudi Arabia's kit mirrored their flag: white shirts trimmed in green at home, green trimmed in white away, unchanged across six World Cups from 1994 to 2022. In 2023, incoming supplier Adidas flipped the scheme entirely, making green the new home color and white the away, with gold trim drawn from the palm tree on the national emblem — the biggest kit shake-up in the federation's history, arriving one tournament too late for Qatar 2022.

1994.headline: The Solo Run That Stunned Belgium

annotations.label: THE SOLO RUN
annotations.story: Al-Owairan picked up the ball inside his own half, surged past three Belgian defenders and beat goalkeeper Michel Preud'homme with a low finish. It remains the signature moment of Saudi Arabia's World Cup history, still replayed as one of the tournament's greatest individual goals three decades later.

annotations.label: HISTORY IN THE MEADOWLANDS
annotations.story: Saudi Arabia's 2–1 win over Morocco in the Meadowlands did more than send them second in the group — it made them the first Asian side ever to beat an African team at a World Cup, a footnote that still stands as this tournament remains one of Asia's finest showings.

annotations.label: SHAMEL ON GREEN
annotations.story: Saudi Arabia's all-green 1994 shirt carried a patch from Shamel, a domestic sponsor, printed onto the golden generation's simplest-ever kit design. Decades on, collectors regard that jersey as one of the most sought-after retro shirts from Gulf football, prized above the team's more heavily branded shirts of the 2000s.

1994.homeColors: white shirt with green trim, white shorts, white socks (Shamel)
1994.awayColors: green shirt with white trim, green shorts, green socks (Shamel)

1998.headline: Zidane Sees Red

annotations.label: ZIDANE'S RED CARD
annotations.story: Hosts and eventual champions France beat Saudi Arabia 4–0, but the game's lasting image was Zinedine Zidane's dismissal in the 71st minute for stamping on captain Fuad Anwar during a challenge. It was a rare black mark on Zidane's tournament and a strange footnote to one of Saudi Arabia's heaviest World Cup defeats.

annotations.label: THE LATE LEVELER
annotations.story: Trailing South Africa in Bordeaux, Yousuf Al-Thunayan converted a 74th-minute penalty to complete a come-from-behind 2–2 draw — Saudi Arabia's only point of the 1998 tournament. Both Saudi goals that day came from the spot, Sami Al-Jaber leveling first just before halftime.

annotations.label: DRI-FIT DEBUT
annotations.story: Nike took over as Saudi Arabia's kit supplier around France '98, introducing Dri-FIT moisture-wicking fabric to the national team's jersey for the first time. The technical upgrade arrived in the same tournament remembered instead for Zidane's stamp on captain Fuad Anwar.

1998.homeColors: white shirt with green trim, white shorts, white socks
1998.awayColors: green shirt with white trim, green shorts, green socks

2002.headline: Germany's Record Rout

annotations.label: THE 8–0 RECORD
annotations.story: Miroslav Klose scored a hat-trick as Germany put eight goals past Saudi Arabia in Sapporo, a scoreline that still stands as the joint-heaviest defeat in World Cup history. Losses to Cameroon and the Republic of Ireland followed, completing a group stage without a single goal scored.

annotations.label: QUIET KIT DESIGN
annotations.story: Adidas dressed Saudi Arabia at Japan/South Korea 2002 in a predominantly white shirt with green trim at the collar and cuffs, plus the brand's three stripes across the shoulders, a clean design that got little attention next to the historic scorelines racked up against it.

annotations.label: THE COACH PAYS
annotations.story: Coach Nasser Al-Johar, who had guided the team through qualifying, was dismissed soon after the tournament closed with zero goals scored and twelve conceded across three games, the heaviest World Cup collapse by an Asian side since 1954.

2002.homeColors: white shirt with green trim, white shorts, white socks
2002.awayColors: green shirt with white trim, green shorts, green socks

2006.headline: Al-Jaber's Farewell Strike

annotations.label: TWELVE YEARS APART
annotations.story: Sami Al-Jaber's 84th-minute strike against Tunisia leveled the score at 2–2 and made him one of only a handful of players — alongside Pelé, Maradona, Klose, Messi and Ronaldo — to score at World Cups twelve years apart, bookending his career between the USA in 1994 and Germany in 2006.

annotations.label: ITALIAN JOB
annotations.story: Italian brand Lotto supplied Saudi Arabia's kit at Germany 2006, one of several manufacturer changes the federation cycled through in the two decades between Adidas at Argentina '94 and Nike's long tenure beginning in the 2010s.

annotations.label: THE BRAZILIAN GAMBLE
annotations.story: Saudi Arabia turned to Brazilian coach Marcos Paqueta, hired just months before the tournament after he impressed at club side Al-Hilal, betting an outsider could reverse over a decade of decline since the 1994 golden generation. A single point was the return on that bet.

2006.homeColors: white shirt with green trim, white shorts, white socks (Lotto)
2006.awayColors: green shirt with white trim, green shorts, green socks (Lotto)

2018.headline: Al-Dawsari's Last-Gasp Winner

annotations.label: OPENING NIGHT HORROR
annotations.story: Yury Gazinsky's early header opened the floodgates as host nation Russia routed Saudi Arabia 5–0 in the tournament opener at Luzhniki Stadium, Saudi Arabia's second-heaviest defeat in World Cup history after the 8–0 loss to Germany in 2002.

annotations.label: STOPPAGE-TIME WINNER
annotations.story: Trailing Egypt in the closing seconds of their final group game, Salem Al-Dawsari controlled a bouncing ball and volleyed home in the fifth minute of stoppage time, sealing a 2–1 win that was Saudi Arabia's first World Cup victory since Al-Owairan's wonder goal 24 years earlier.

annotations.label: TWELVE YEARS AWAY
annotations.story: The trip to Russia ended a twelve-year absence from the World Cup, Saudi Arabia having missed both South Africa 2010 and Brazil 2014 after failed qualifying campaigns. The team then drew the unenviable assignment of opening the entire tournament, walking out against the hosts in Moscow's Luzhniki Stadium.

2018.homeColors: white shirt with green trim, white shorts, white socks (Nike) — an homage to the 1994 round-of-16 side
2018.awayColors: green shirt and shorts with white trim, green socks (Nike)

2022.headline: The Upset of the Century

annotations.label: THE COMEBACK
annotations.story: Down 1–0 to Lionel Messi's penalty, Saudi Arabia scored twice in four second-half minutes — Saleh Al-Shehri's low finish in the 48th, then Salem Al-Dawsari's curled strike in the 53rd — to beat eventual champions Argentina 2–1. King Salman declared a public holiday back home; it remains regarded as one of the greatest upsets in World Cup history.

annotations.label: ONE MORE FROM DAWSARI
annotations.story: Al-Dawsari struck again in stoppage time against Mexico, curling home from a one-two, but this time it wasn't enough — Saudi Arabia's 1–2 defeat, combined with Poland's superior goal difference, sent them home in the group stage despite their earlier heroics against Argentina.

annotations.label: FEATHERS OF THE FALCON
annotations.story: Nike's 2022 home shirt printed a pale grey falcon-feather graphic across the front, a nod to the saqr crest of the Saudi football federation, while the away kit's all-over green print was built to evoke speed, a design language the brand had been developing across the team's kits since 2018.

2022.homeColors: white shirt with green collar and Swoosh, palm-leaf pattern, white shorts and socks (Nike)
2022.awayColors: dark green shirt with white logos and an all-over tonal pattern, green shorts and socks (Nike)
